input.initialPrompt: Before creating anything, show me exactly what files would be generated if I create a Blazor Web App called MyBlazorApp in ./test-output with interactive server rendering. Don't actually create the project yet.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

data.reasoningText: The user wants to know what files would be generated by creating a Blazor Web App called "MyBlazorApp" with interactive server rendering, without actually creating it. I can use `dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server --dry-run` to show what would be generated without actually creating files.

arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server --dry-run 2>&1", "description": "Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity", "initial_wait": 15}

toolArgs.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

data.content:
Here's what would be generated. The key application files:

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `Components/App.razor` | Root app component |
| `Components/Routes.razor` | Routing setup |
| `Components/_Imports.razor` | Global using directives |
| `Components/Layout/` | MainLayout, NavMenu, ReconnectModal (+ CSS/JS) |
| `Components/Pages/` | Counter, Error, Home, NotFound, Weather |
| `Program.cs` | App entry point & service config |
| `MyBlazorApp.csproj` | Project file |
| `Properties/launchSettings.json` | Dev launch profiles |
| `appsettings*.json` | Configuration |
| `wwwroot/app.css` | App styles |
| `wwwroot/favicon.png` | Favicon |
| `wwwroot/lib/bootstrap/` | Bootstrap CSS & JS (grid, reboot, utilities, bundle — ~30 files with maps) |

**Total: ~67 files.** The bulk is Bootstrap assets. A NuGet restore would also run automatically after creation.

The command to actually create it would be:
```
dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server
```
